symptom: NCCL Ring algorithm deadlock on A100 clusters with >32 nodes
notes: Quarantined from the old flat rules.py (NCCL-RING-001). No source was ever recorded for this claim - it predates the mining pipeline. Needs a real Prompt A pass to find supporting evidence (or be dropped if none exists). Do not promote as-is: it fails the schema's own mandatory source_url/symptom-with-citation gate.

symptom: AllReduce data corruption on H100 when NIC shares PCI switch with one GPU
notes: Actual failure additionally requires a specific PCI topology (a NIC sharing a PCI switch with exactly one GPU) that traincheck cannot detect statically - if promoted, should be WARN with a topology caveat in the message, not a hard ERROR. Workaround per NVIDIA: NCCL_PROTO=^LL128 or NCCL_IB_PCI_RELAXED_ORDERING=0.

symptom: DistBackendError NCCL error with PyTorch Lightning DDP/DP strategy
notes: SKIPPED at extraction, recorded only for the audit trail: thread has multiple conflicting unconfirmed theories (pytorch 2.1.0 regression, CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES ordering, an unrelated USB-NIC bug fixed in 2.20.x per #1125), no linked fix commit, and was auto-closed by a stale-issue bot (not 'completed'). Does not meet the closed-with-linked-fix bar. Do not re-mine without a clearer, single-cause report.

symptom: ZeRO-3 + PEFT LoRA bf16 base model: TypeError dtype mismatch in _allgather_params_coalesced

fixed_in.version: unreleased as of mining date - merged to main via PR #8073 (commit b5b3fded), no tagged release after 0.19.2 yet contains it

trigger_field: sharding (ZeRO stage) + peft/lora usage (not tracked) + bf16 enabled (not tracked)
notes: Real, well-documented regression with a merged fix PR (#8073, confirmed merged=true via API) - but traincheck has no PEFT/LoRA usage extraction at all, and no field for a DeepSpeed config's own bf16.enabled flag, so the trigger can't be evaluated even though sharding (ZeRO stage 3) alone is visible. Confidence capped at medium rather than high: the fix is merged to main but not yet in any tagged release as of the mining date, so there is no 'upgrade to X' remediation to give a user yet. Re-mine once a release ships and/or if PEFT/LoRA detection is ever added.

symptom: FSDP2 mixed-precision fp32 upcast is a silent no-op, trains in bf16 master weights
notes: The GitHub issue itself was closed by a stale-bot with an unanswered 'is this fixed?' comment - NOT sufficient alone. Verified independently by diffing accelerate's own source history: buggy `param = param.to(torch.float32)` introduced by PR #3905 (merged 2026-01-13, first in v1.13.0) was reverted to the correct `param.data = param.data.to(torch.float32)` by PR #3985 (merged 2026-04-23, first in v1.14.0), which also added a regression test asserting fp32 master weights. Bug is real, silent (no error, no crash - just wrong-precision training), and precisely bounded to exactly accelerate==1.13.0. Not expressible: traincheck has no field for the accelerate package's own pip-installed version (only framework_version/framework_name for the training framework, and dependency_constraints which is only wired for Ray). Would become directly expressible (mixed_precision != 'no' and accelerate_version == (1,13,0)) if an accelerate-version field were added, e.g. parsed from a requirements.txt/lockfile pin the way Ray's dependency_constraints already works.
